Description: Create a console application that prompts the user to enter two numbers: a)    Integer (can be positive or negative) and b)    Base-N (must be positive)
The app should iteratively ensure that the base-N entered is positive, i.e., re-prompt if a negative or zero is entered.
The app should break down the given integer into base-N digits by repeatedly fetching the remainders as Base-N digits and dividing the integer by N.
Then, the app then calculates and displays the sum of all these Base-N digits.
The app also determines the largest Base-N digit
Create and test a method that accepts an array.
The method returns the count array with the same length as the original array.
In a nested loop, the method then counts the number of insertions necessary for each element and store them into the count array.
Implement it in O(n^2) time without fully implementing the insertion sort algorithm.
Create and test a method that accepts an array as input.
The method should then count the occurrence of each number in the array and store them into a dictionary.
Next, the method iterates through the dictionary to identify all the largest numbers for different frequencies.
Finally, it should sum up all the products of these values with their corresponding frequencies to determine the “sum of all the largest numbers repeated in every different frequency.”
Return 0 when the input is array is invalid (i.e., null).
To achieve O(n) time complexity, we can use two dictionaries: one for counting the occurrences and another for tracking the largest value for different frequencies
